response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced full-stack engineer with strong testing culture and teaching credentials, but the profile diverges sharply from this role's core requirements. The position demands 8 years of C++ and 5 years building large-scale infrastructure or compute platforms; the resume shows roughly a year of professional C/C++ (National Instruments, thesis work) with the bulk of the career in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web product development. The Sociomantic low-latency systems work is the closest match but is D-based and about two years long. There is also no formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers, and the recent career gap is minor context rather than a driver of the decision.
strengths: High-throughput, low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic (millions of requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source test tooling, and technical mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad polyglot range across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with Docker/Kubernetes exposure
concerns: Far short of the 8-year C++ requirement — only ~1 year of professional C/C++ work, a decade ago
No production cloud compute platform or large-scale distributed infrastructure ownership; background is product/web engineering
No formal technical-lead role managing cross-team priorities or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers
